channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: State Department freezes new funding for nearly all US aid programs worldwide
item.description: The State Department has frozen new funding for almost all U.S. aid programs worldwide, making exceptions to allow humanitarian food programs and military aid to Israel and Egypt to continue.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal agencies begin removing DEI guidance from websites in Trump crackdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Several federal agencies have begun removing resources for underrepresented Americans from their webpages following President Donald Trump's executive order cracking down on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: ALIA WONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Measure to make California an independent country cleared to gather signatures
item.description: Another ballot measure asking whether California should be its own country is cleared to start gathering signatures, the California Secretary of State announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Mariann Budde, the bishop who upset Trump with her inaugural sermon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Right Rev. Mariann Budde made headlines this week after she angered President Donald Trump with her sermon during an inaugural prayer service.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he wants to overhaul FEMA as he travels to North Carolina and California
item.description: President Trump is taking the first trip of his term on Friday to North Carolina and California, visiting communities grappling with recovery from natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid and Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: 1,300 arrested in big city deportation operations: Homan
item.description: Number in first three days of Trump’s presidency represents about 1.1% of total arrests made by ICE in FY2024.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Texas Senate’s voucher proposal would give families $10,000 to fund students’ private school
item.description: Senate Bill 2 starts a new debate on a school voucher program in Texas. A funding proposal would allow up to 100,000 K-12 students who want to enroll to a private school to participate.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump and Vance join March for Life anti-abortion activists in celebrating the movement's gains
item.description: President Donald Trump and his vice president, JD Vance, are celebrating gains in the anti-abortion movement with activists in the annual March for Life.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Christine Fernando And Ashraf Khalil, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Who's the guy handing Trump those binders of executive orders? Meet Will Scharf
item.description: Those binders full of executive orders that President Donald Trump has been signing with a flourish in recent days don’t just magically appear before him.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Nevada state senator takes on gender, sports issue at UNLV, UNR, community colleges
item.description: Republican state Senator Carrie Buck is sponsoring legislation that would require Nevada colleges and universities to restrict participation in athletics based on the athlete's gender at birth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: The new Justice Department leadership has put a freeze on civil rights litigation, and suggested it may reconsider police reform agreements negotiated by the Biden administration. That's according to two memos obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democrats weighing limits on presidential pardons after Trump, Biden moves
item.description: House Democrats are calling for Congress to rein in the president’s pardon powers after President Trump’s mass clemency for the Jan. 6 mob and President Biden’s reprieve for certain family members.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests it was a mistake Biden didn't pardon himself
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday repeatedly suggested it was a mistake that former President Biden did not preemptively pardon himself before leaving office. Trump, in a sit-down interview with Fox News host Sean Hannity, also danced around questions on whether Biden should be investigated.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls DEI programs ‘illegal’ and ‘immoral.’ Here’s how he’s ending them
item.description: President Donald Trump has issued sweeping executive actions swiftly ending diversity, equity, inclusion and accessibility programs within the federal government. Already, the work is underway.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Meta denies platforms are forcing users to follow Trump, Vance
item.description: "Those accounts are managed by the White House so with a new administration, the content on those Pages changes," Andy Stone wrote.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Federal health agencies told to halt all external communications
item.description: In a memo obtained by NPR, acting Health Secretary Dorothy Fink forbade staff from public communications on most matters until Feb. 1, unless they get express approval from "a presidential appointee.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel, Selena Simmons-Duffin, and Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump wants to pull US out of World Health Organization again; here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday, including a top envoy to Iran during his first term and retired Gen. Mark Milley, who Trump tapped as Joint Chiefs chairman in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Why Biden pardoned Mark Milley ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's pre-emptive pardon for retired Gen. Mark Milley, the former chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give the retired military official a shield against any action that President Donald Trump might take against him amid their highly public feud.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Ramaswamy won't serve on Trump's DOGE as he mulls run for Ohio governor
item.description: Ramaswamy, who sought the Republican nomination for president in 2024, has signaled plans to run for governor of Ohio.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NJ governor sends letter to Trump seeking to end congestion pricing
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy sent a letter to President Donald Trump on Monday seeking to end congestion pricing in New York City. Congestion pricing went into effect on Jan. 5.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, PIX 11

item.title: Special session rebuttal on illegal immigration continues in Florida
item.description: The governor gave his order Monday with House and Senate leadership pushing back, and that's not the only rebuttal we've seen in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
